title: Psalm CXVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O praise the Lord, all ye heathen: praise Him, all ye nations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the courts of the Lord’s house, this all His people, this the true Jerusalem.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those rather listen who have refused to be the children of this city, since they have cut themselves off from the communion of all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For His merciful kindness is ever more and more towards us: and the truth of the Lord endureth for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are those two things, loving-kindness and truth, which in the CXVth Psalm I admonished you should be committed to memory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “the merciful kindness of the Lord is ever more and more towards us,” since the furious tongues of hostile nations have yielded to His Name, through which we have been freed: “and the truth of the Lord endureth for ever,” whether in those things which He promised to the righteous, or in those which He hath threatened to the ungodly.
